title: Configure device access control using local passwords

questions.question: Which command configure the enable password for a router or switch?

correct.explanation: The correct answer is "Router(config)#enable secret Password20!".

incorrect.explanation: Router(config)#password enable Password20! does not match the mechanism asked here.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Router(config)#password enable Password2…" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Router(config)#enable Password20! does not match the mechanism asked here.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Router(config)#enable Password20!" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Router(config)#secret enable Password20! does not match the mechanism asked here.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Router(config)#secret enable Password20!" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: Re-read the stem constraint for local device access before picking a familiar-sounding wrong term.

questions.question: You need to set the login password for Telnet. Which command you type first?

correct.explanation: The correct answer is "Switch(config)#line vty 0 5".

incorrect.explanation: Switch(config)#interface vlan 1 does not match the mechanism asked here.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Switch(config)#interface vlan 1" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Switch(config)#line console 1 does not match the mechanism asked here.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Switch(config)#line console 1" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Switch(config)#line aux 1 does not match the mechanism asked here.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Switch(config)#line aux 1" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: Cleartext Telnet fails security stems — SSH is the management answer.

questions.question: You have set the enable password using enable password Password20!. However, when you try to get to a privileged exec prompt, the router states that you are using an incorrect password. What is the problem?
choices: You originally entered the wrong password.
The enable secret password is set to something else.
The password Password20! contains a special character.
The password is too long and has been truncated.

correct.explanation: The correct answer is "The enable secret password is set to something else.".

incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "The password is too long and has been tr…" without matching the scenario constraint

questions.question: Which command configure the router or switch to allow SSH as a protocol for management with a fallback of Telnet?

correct.explanation: The correct answer is "Switch(config-line)#transport ssh telnet".

incorrect.explanation: Telnet sends credentials in cleartext — SSH is required for secure device management.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Picking Telnet when secure management is required

incorrect.explanation: Switch(config-line)#login ssh telnet is not the behavior described here. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Picking Telnet when secure management is required

incorrect.explanation: Switch(config)#transport ssh telnet is not the behavior described here. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Picking Telnet when secure management is required

answerReview.examTip: Cleartext Telnet fails security stems — SSH is the management answer.

questions.question: Why should Telnet be replaced with SSH?
choices: SSH makes it easier to create ACLs for access.

correct.explanation: The correct answer is "SSH is encrypted.".

incorrect.explanation: Telnet sends credentials in cleartext — SSH is required for secure device management.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Picking Telnet when secure management is required

incorrect.explanation: SSH allows for file copy. does not match the mechanism asked here.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "SSH allows for file copy." without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: SSH makes it easier to create ACLs for access. reflects a common trap: ACLs encrypt or deeply inspect traffic. ACLs filter by header fields (IP/protocol/port); they are not encryption or stateful firewalls.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: ACLs encrypt or deeply inspect traffic

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: Forgetting the implicit deny — An ACL with only permits blocks everything else via the implicit deny any

questions.question: Which command create and apply an access list to secure router or switch management?
choices: Switch(config)#access-list 1 permit host 192.168.1.5 Switch(config)#interface vlan 1 Switch(config-if)#ip access-group 1 in
Switch(config)#access-list 1 permit host 192.168.1.5 Switch(config)#line vty 0 5 Switch(config-line)#ip access-group 1 in
Switch(config)#access-list 1 permit host 192.168.1.5 Switch(config)#line vty 0 5 Switch(config-line)#ip access-class 1 in
Switch(config)#access-list 1 permit host 192.168.1.5 Switch(config)#ip access-group 1 in

correct.explanation: The correct answer is "Switch(config)#access-list 1 permit host 192.168.1.5 Switch(config)#line vty 0 5 Switch(config-line)#ip access-class 1 in".

incorrect.explanation: Switch(config)#access-list 1 permit host 192.168.1.5 Switch(config)#interface vlan 1 Switch(config-if)#ip access-group 1 in does not match the mechanism asked here.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Switch(config)#access-list 1 permit host…" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Switch(config)#access-list 1 permit host 192.168.1.5 Switch(config)#line vty 0 5 Switch(config-line)#ip access-group 1 in does not match the mechanism asked here.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Switch(config)#access-list 1 permit host…" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Switch(config)#access-list 1 permit host 192.168.1.5 Switch(config)#ip access-group 1 in does not match the mechanism asked here.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Switch(config)#access-list 1 permit host…" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: Forgetting the implicit deny — An ACL with only permits blocks everything else via the implicit deny any

questions.question:

*  0 con 0                idle                 0          local

   2 vty 1                idle                 5          local
